name: De'Von Achane
headline: Elite chunk-play back facing a thinner receiving menu
body:
Achane's 2025 spine is loud: 1,350 rushing yards on 5.67 a carry, an explosive-run rate near 17% and 22 broken tackles, plus 3.02 yards after contact per attempt across 16 games. He finished RB6 with 255.8 fantasy points, and the dual-threat profile is what separates him — 67 catches, 488 receiving yards and a healthy 18.4% target share on 381 routes. A 75.4% snap share confirms he's the unquestioned engine of this backfield.

The regression case is real but narrow. A new play-caller and mobile quarterback Malik Willis figure to siphon checkdowns, trimming the receiving volume that anchors his PPR floor, and goal-line work has never been a strength. An offseason shoulder procedure shouldn't cost camp time. He's a back-end RB1 — mid-second-round value around the RB8-9 range — with a thinner ceiling than his talent suggests.
